questions.q: All clerks in the office are college graduates. Rosa is a clerk in the office. Therefore:
options: Rosa may be a college graduate
Rosa is not a college graduate
questions.explanation: Valid syllogism: all clerks are graduates, Rosa is a clerk, so Rosa is a graduate.

questions.q: Find the next in the series: A, C, F, J, O, ___
questions.explanation: Gaps grow by one letter: +2, +3, +4, +5, then +6 → T.

questions.q: Five people sit in a row. Jo is not at either end. Ben is at the far left. Mia sits beside Ben. Who is second from the left?
questions.explanation: Ben is leftmost and Mia is beside him, so Mia is second from the left.

questions.q: If the statement "All reports were submitted on time" is FALSE, which must be TRUE?
options: No reports were submitted on time
At least one report was not submitted on time
questions.explanation: The negation of "all" is "at least one was not" — not "none" or "all late."

questions.q: Pedro is taller than Juan. Juan is taller than Luis. Carlo is taller than Pedro. Who is shortest?
questions.explanation: Order: Carlo > Pedro > Juan > Luis. Luis is shortest.
All are odd, but 9 is the only one that is not a prime number.

questions.q: A code shifts each letter forward by 2 (A→C). What is the code for "BAG"?
If today is Wednesday, what day will it be 100 days from now?
questions.explanation: 100 ÷ 7 leaves remainder 2, so two days after Wednesday is Friday.

questions.q: Statement: Some government employees are teachers. All teachers are licensed. Which conclusion follows?
questions.explanation: The employees who are teachers must be licensed, so some employees are licensed.
Fibonacci: each number is the sum of the previous two; 5 + 8 = 13.
